authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Witch's Pond not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Witch's Pond not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Witch's Pond not listed as a stocked water)

parameters.note: Witch's Pond is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1), which expressly covers ponds 'including those on private lands.' It is a very small (~0.1-acre / ~530 m²) pond at the south end of Fallen Leaf Lake, within the grounds of Stanford Sierra Camp (El Dorado Co.), above Fallen Leaf Lake — so it is not itself a Lake Tahoe tributary and the §7.50(b)(152) tributary closure does not apply. No CDFW stocking record or species source names any fish in it (the 2026 Provisional Stocking Document does not list it), so species and stocking are left empty. Public/physical access is via the private camp; no authoritative access rule is asserted here.
